• Consistent output across every image, leaving no obvious signs of retouching.  
• Final files returned in the original resolution (JPEG/PNG as supplied), ready for immediate use online or in print.

The ideal candidate will be able to: Review and cull a full gallery of RAW images Thoughtfully narrow the gallery down to the requested final image count Select images that align with my photography style, prioritizing: Genuine connection Authentic emotion Love, warmth, and storytelling Natural interactions and meaningful moments Edit images in a way that is consistent with my established brand aesthetic Maintain natural skin tones, cohesive color grading, and a polished, timeless look Deliver completed galleries within 48 hours of receiving the files Culling: My work focuses on emotive family and portrait photography and human connection, so I'm looking for someone who understands that the strongest image isn't always the most technically perfect one—it's often the image that best captures connection, feeling, and authenticity. When culling, I want someone who can recognize the subtle moments—a hand squeeze, a genuine laugh, a child leaning into a parent, a quiet glance—that often become the most meaningful images in a gallery. I'll always need a handful of traditional portraits where everyone is looking at the camera, but overall, technically perfect images should not be selected over images that tell a stronger emotional story. My editing style is warm, emotive, and timeless, with rich yet natural color tones. I prioritize authentic connection, genuine emotion, and storytelling over perfectly posed images. Edits should feel polished and artistic while remaining true-to-life, preserving natural skin tones, beautiful light, and the emotional integrity of each moment. My work is designed to evoke feeling—capturing love, joy, tenderness, and the quiet in-between moments that tell a family's story: Words to describe my brand: Emotive Storytelling Fine art Warm and inviting Rich tones Authentic Heartfelt Connection-focused Timeless Organic Natural light Genuine emotion Love notes for the soul (your branding phrase) Requirements: -Experience editing family, lifestyle, or portrait photography -Strong culling instincts and storytelling ability -Proficiency in Adobe Lightroom (Photoshop experience is a plus) -Ability to closely match an existing editing style -Reliable communication and consistent turnaround times When applying, please include: Portfolio examples of family or portrait work Examples of galleries you've culled and edited Your typical turnaround time Your rate structure (per gallery or hourly) I'm looking to build a long-term relationship with the right person as my business continues to grow.
task_type: Batch Style-Match Grade for Portrait Gallery (Culling is Human)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (the selected family/portrait images) -> image_list_presets / image_apply_preset (apply the photographer's warm-emotive look across the set for consistency) -> image_apply_auto_tone (per-frame baseline) -> image_adjust_color_temperature + image_adjust_hsl + image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (rich-yet-natural tones, true-to-life skin) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The chosen final selects to grade (client culls down to a requested count from a full RAW gallery — selection itself is human)
The photographer's base preset / a reference edited gallery that defines the 'warm, emotive, timeless, rich-but-natural' look to match
Target export specs + 48-hour turnaround expectation per gallery
Confirmation skin tones must stay natural and untouched-looking
note: Connector applies the consistent warm grade/tone across the gallery; the core culling/selection (judging emotional moments) and precise style-matching to the photographer's hand are human.

Meta ads — create static ad creative across multiple sizes for ongoing paid social campaigns. Landing pages & web assets. Sales enablement — pitch decks, one-pagers, PDF assets. Social content — LinkedIn graphics, YouTube thumbnails. Expert-level Figma. 3+ years in marketing or brand design for B2B SaaS.
task_type: Resize / Reformat — Multi-size Ad Creative (from existing master)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload an approved master ad creative) -> image_crop_and_resize (produce each required Meta/social ad size) -> image_generative_expand (extend canvas when an aspect ratio needs more background) -> asset_preview_file

title: Print Designer Wanted – Ongoing Work, Real Art, High Standards
desc: We need a detail-obsessed print designer to refine our artwork and make it print-ready across multiple formats. This is ongoing work for the right person. Garbage Gallery is an independent art gallery and poster brand. We take art seriously — but not ourselves. We release limited edition art posters that live somewhere between fine art and street culture. What You'll Be Doing Refining and cleaning up art posters in Adobe Illustrator Preparing print-ready files (bleed, color profiles, resolution, crop marks) Adapting designs across standard poster sizes (A4, A3, A2, A1, 50x70, 70x100, etc.) Ensuring color accuracy for both digital and offset print What We're Looking For Solid print production experience — you know your way around CMYK, bleed and print specs Adobe Illustrator is your primary tool Pixel-perfect attention to detail Able to work independently with minimal hand-holding This Is Ongoing Work If we click, this turns into a long-term collaboration. We value people who take pride in clean, precise work. To Apply Send your portfolio — show us print work, poster production, or anything that proves you care about the details.
task_type: Poster Resize / Reformat Across Print Sizes — Garbage Gallery
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (the existing poster artwork files) -> image_crop_and_resize to each standard poster size (A4, A3, A2, A1, 50x70, 70x100) -> image_generative_expand where a target ratio needs extra canvas at the edges -> document_render_vector / document_convert_pdf (export print PDF per size) -> asset_preview_file

The full target size list: A4, A3, A2, A1, 50x70, 70x100 (and any others)
Print spec details to confirm — bleed amount, CMYK profile, crop marks (brief requires these but connector can't set them)
Whether each poster needs all sizes or a subset
note: Connector resizes/reframes each poster to the size set and exports PDFs; true print-prep (bleed, crop marks, CMYK ICC profile) and the manual Illustrator 'clean-up' of the artwork are human.

title: Menu Design for Restaurant
desc: We are seeking a creative designer to craft a visually appealing menu for our restaurant. The ideal candidate will have experience in menu design and a strong understanding of design principles. Responsibilities include creating a layout that is both functional and aesthetically pleasing, ensuring the menu effectively communicates our offerings to customers. The project requires a quick turnaround, with the final design delivered in PDF format.

mcp_workflow: search_design (find a pre-made Express restaurant-menu template) -> fill_text (swap in the restaurant's menu items/prices) -> document_convert_pdf (export final menu as PDF) -> asset_preview_file

Restaurant name and any brand colors/logo to drop in
Final output: PDF (confirm page size, e.g. A4 / US Letter / tri-fold)
Style direction / any reference menu the client likes

title: Redesign of Product Brochures
desc: Brochure Design & Brand Template Creation for Engineering / Industrial Company We are looking for an experienced graphic designer to redesign and modernise a suite of brochures for Polymax, one of the UK’s leading manufacturers and distributors of rubber solutions and engineering products. The project forms part of a wider rebrand alongside the launch of a new contemporary website, so the brochure designs must align closely with the new digital brand style. Scope of Work The project includes: Redesign of existing brochures Creation of a new corporate brochure Design of multiple new product family brochures Creation of editable brochure templates for future internal use Development of a consistent visual style system across all brochures Creation of a set of custom icons for use in both brochures and on the website One brochure family (O-rings) will require: A marketing-focused version A more technical version with expanded engineering information These must be visually differentiated while remaining within the same overall brand system. Design Requirements The brochures should: Reflect the styling of the new website Be modern, technical, professional and visually engaging Appeal primarily to engineers, technical buyers and industrial distributors Use strong imagery with concise copy Incorporate icons, tables, bullet points and clean layouts Be adaptable for use by a sister company (alternative colour variants and logos) The initial brochure format will be: A5 Print-ready and digital PDF formats Deliverables The successful freelancer will provide: Print-ready PDF artwork Adobe InDesign packaged files with bleed setup Editable InDesign templates SVG and PNG icon assets created during the project Assets Provided We will supply: Existing brochures Draft brochure content Product information Brand guidance Images and graphics Icon requirements list Working Style This will be a collaborative and iterative project with several rounds of feedback. The Marketing Manager will act as the main point of contact throughout the project. Ideal Freelancer We are looking for someone with: Strong brochure/editorial design experience Experience creating corporate or industrial marketing materials Excellent typography and layout skills Ability to create clean technical visuals/icons Strong Adobe InDesign skills Attention to detail and consistency across multiple documents Experience working with engineering, manufacturing or industrial brands would be highly beneficial. Timeline Target completion dates: Corporate, O-Rings & Gaskets brochures: 26 June 2026 AV Mounts & Mouldings brochures: 17 July 2026 Site Safety, Fenders, Silicone & Technical O-Ring brochures: 31 July 2026
task_type: Data-Merge Brochure Layout + Icon Vectorize (Polymax, A5)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (upload Polymax product data, supplied images, draft copy) -> document_merge_data_layout (populate a templated A5 brochure layout from the product data for repeated product-family pages) -> image_vectorize (rebuild supplied raster icon sketches into clean vectors) -> document_render_vector (export icon SVG + PNG asset set) -> document_convert_pdf (produce print-ready + digital PDF) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: Supplied assets: existing brochures, draft brochure content, product information, brand guidance, images/graphics, icon requirements list
Format: A5, print-ready + digital PDF; alternate colour/logo variants for the sister company
Icon set required as SVG + PNG (vectorize from the supplied icon source sketches)
Note: original icon design from scratch, bespoke InDesign template design, and the new visual-style system are NOT connector-doable
note: Connector can data-merge product info into a templated layout, convert to print/digital PDF, and vectorize supplied icon sketches to SVG/PNG, but it cannot design the bespoke brand-aligned brochure system, author editable InDesign templates, or originate custom icons from a requirements list; the core creative design needs a human.

title: Nagpur Silver Jewelry Photoshoot
desc:
I have about 2,000 pieces of 925 sterling-silver jewellery that now need clean, consistent imagery for our online catalogue and marketplaces. All shots must be taken here in Nagpur, preferably in a simple studio set-up so the metalwork stays the hero and lighting is fully controlled.

For most pieces a plain white sweep is enough, but a few designs could benefit from a quick prop to help them stand upright—simple jewellery stands or holders will do. If a piece already balances well on its own, no prop is required; I’ll decide on the day which ones need the extra support.

After shooting, each file has to be delivered on pure #FFFFFF with no visible shadows or colour cast. I’m comfortable if you run the clean-up through an AI workflow, Photoshop, or Lightroom—as long as the final images are crisp, evenly exposed and ready for immediate upload. High-resolution JPEGs (3,000 px on the long edge) plus the corresponding layered PSDs are the deliverables I expect.

Because volume is high, batch processing skills and an organised naming convention are essential. Share a quick sample workflow or a before/after if you have one shot on an iPhone; smartphone capture is perfectly fine as long as the finished result meets standard jewellery e-commerce quality.

I’d like a realistic timeline and checkpoint plan—for example, a small pilot batch first, followed by rolling deliveries until all 2,000 pieces are complete. Let me know your availability to start in Nagpur and how many finished images you can comfortably supply per week.
task_type: Product Photo Cleanup — Pure-White Jewelry Catalog (batch)
mcp_workflow: asset_create_folders (one intake/output set, organised naming for the ~2000 pieces) -> asset_add_file (ingest each shot JPEG, looped) -> image_apply_auto_tone (even exposure, remove colour cast on the silver) -> image_remove_background (clean cut-out of each piece, kills shadows) -> image_fill_area on the inverted/empty background with #FFFFFF (guarantee pure white, no cast) [alt: change_background_color to #FFFFFF] -> image_crop_and_resize (3000 px long edge, consistent framing) -> asset_preview_file (pilot-batch checkpoint before rolling deliveries)
inputs: The studio-shot product JPEGs (~2,000 pieces of 925 sterling-silver jewellery; capture is the client/photographer's job, not the connector's)
Exact output spec already stated: pure #FFFFFF background, no visible shadow/colour cast, 3,000 px on the long edge, high-res JPEG
Naming convention / SKU mapping for the organised batch
Pilot-batch size for first checkpoint (client wants a small pilot then rolling deliveries — confirm count)
Whether any pieces are shot on prop stands that must also be cut away cleanly
note: Connector only handles post (white-bg cleanup/resize); the photoshoot in Nagpur is out of scope, and it cannot deliver the requested layered PSDs — only flattened JPEG/PNG.

title: Photo Restoration
desc: Digitize 1910's postcard photo's front and back. Approx 24 photos. These postcards were taken back in 1910 of a famed magician and his traveling company in Asia and abroad. They company included my Grandmother and Aunt, they both described the picture, on the back side of the post card photo. I would like to have the front as one picture and the back side as another . The picture (some) are dark and would like for them to be lightened and clear. The writing on the back of each photo needs to be clear and legible. I need the finish work to be electronicall capabile to be copied, pasted in a book, and social media - the book will be created after this project is completed. A couple of examples are attached.
task_type: Old Photo Enhancement — Lighten & Clarify 1910s Postcards (front+back)
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (the ~24 scanned postcard fronts + ~24 backs = ~48 images) -> image_apply_auto_tone -> image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast + image_adjust_dark_portions (lighten the dark cards) -> image_adjust_highlights (make handwriting on the back legible) -> image_crop_to_bounds / image_auto_straighten (clean each card edge) -> asset_preview_file -> deliver front and back as separate files

Per the brief: front kept as one image, back as a separate image — naming/pairing scheme
Confirmation the goal is lighten + clarity + legible handwriting (not heavy damage repair)
Output format suitable to paste into a book + post on social (confirm dimensions/format)
note: Connector lightens, boosts contrast, straightens and crops to make the cards and handwriting legible; physical digitizing/scanning is the client's, and any torn/stained-area repair (clone/heal) would need a human.

title: Horticulture Company Profile With Photos
desc:
I need a fresh, visually engaging company profile that I can send straight to the printer and hand out at trade shows or client meetings. Its primary job is marketing, so every page has to sell our horticulture expertise at a glance.

Content  
• Company history and mission – a concise narrative that highlights our roots, values and long-term vision in sustainable horticulture.  
• Services offered – clear, persuasive copy that outlines greenhouse construction, crop consultation and turnkey project management.  

Imagery  
High-resolution, royalty-free photos are essential. Where possible, weave in shots of our nurseries, equipment in action and finished landscape projects so the reader feels the scale and quality of our work.

Design & Format  
Print-ready, CMYK PDF (and the working file in InDesign or Illustrator) at 300 dpi, A4 size with a 3 mm bleed. Clean typography, plenty of white space and colours that echo natural greens.

Acceptance criteria  
1. Eight to twelve polished pages.  
2. All text proof-read and aligned with UK English.  
3. Image rights documented.  
4. Final files pass a pre-press check (no missing fonts, links or colour-space issues).

If you have a portfolio of similar brochure or profile work, especially in agriculture or horticulture, I’d like to see it before we start so we can lock in a visual direction quickly.
task_type: Stock Asset Sourcing — Horticulture Imagery
mcp_workflow: asset_search (StockAsset, query royalty-free horticulture: nurseries / greenhouses / landscaping) -> asset_license_and_download_stock (license the selected high-res shots, gives rights documentation) -> asset_preview_file (proof the licensed imagery) -> asset_create_folders (collect the licensed image set for handoff to the layout designer)
inputs: List of image themes the client named: nurseries, equipment in action, finished landscape/greenhouse projects (royalty-free, high-res for 300dpi A4 print)
Confirm how many hero/supporting images are needed for the 8–12 page profile
Brand colour direction: natural greens — ask client for exact hex/CMYK if they have brand colours, brief only says 'echo natural greens'
Note the deliverable spec: CMYK, 300dpi, A4, 3mm bleed (drives min stock resolution to license)
note: Connector can source/license the royalty-free imagery and document rights, but cannot do the InDesign/Illustrator multi-page brochure layout, copywriting, or CMYK pre-press — a human designer must build the 8–12 page profile.

title: YouTube Sci-Fi Explainer Editor
desc:
I run a small but steadily growing YouTube channel where I break down Science-Fiction and Fantasy films. Each episode needs brisk, energetic pacing—quick cuts, punchy transitions, and the kind of visual rhythm that keeps viewers glued for every plot twist. I also want subtle animated graphics woven in to highlight key scenes or concepts, giving the recap extra flair without overwhelming the footage.

Here’s how the workflow will look: I supply the raw voice-over, movie clips, and a rough beat sheet. Your task is to craft a polished 8-12-minute edit in 1080p, sync everything to the script, layer in suitable royalty-free SFX or risers if you feel they boost impact, and hand back both the final render and the project file so we can refine future episodes more quickly.

I’m still a student, so the budget starts lean, but consistency is guaranteed—expect a new video every week. As watch time and ad revenue climb, the rate will climb with it. Clear, friendly communication is important to me, and I always keep deadlines realistic so no one scrambles at the last minute.

Deliverables per episode:  
• Final video (H.264, 1080p, 25-30 fps)  
• Project file with all media relinked  
• Any custom animated assets created  

If high-energy edits and futuristic storytelling speak to you, let’s build a channel—and a long-term partnership—together.
task_type: YouTube Explainer — Auto Highlight Cut
mcp_workflow: asset_add_file (movie clips footage) -> video_create_quick_cut (brisk quick-cut assembly toward the 8-12 min recap) -> video_resize (export 1080p 16:9) -> asset_preview_file
inputs: The raw movie clips and rough beat sheet
Target 8-12 min, output H.264 1080p 25-30fps (stated)
The voice-over track + chosen SFX/risers (connector cannot sync VO or place SFX)
Any 'custom animated graphics' the brief wants (connector cannot create motion graphics)
note: Only the quick-cut footage assembly + 1080p export is connector-doable; voice-over sync to script, SFX/risers, the requested custom animated graphics, and the relinked editable project file all need a human in an NLE.
